title: Chinese Consumers Buying More Goods, But Strength Is Lessening
section_path: Chinese Consumers Buying More Goods, But Strength Is Lessening
section_path_text: Chinese Consumers Buying More Goods, But Strength Is Lessening
summary:
Main
By Jeffrey Landsberg
China's retail sales in April grew year-on-year by only 2.3%, which is even lower than the 3.1% growth seen in March. Taking April's 0.3% inflation into account, China's retail sales adjusted for inflation grew year-on-year by only 2%, which marks the lowest growth seen since January 2023. Image reference: Chart.jpg | assets/2024-05-31_5e6rjfxk0fvkss9wnk811q9r3k8o3k_img_chart_a770e1f9fc44.jpg
China's retail sales growth has continued to decline from November's peak.
